channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: After Voting Rights Act case arguments, concerns over diminished minority representation rise
item.description: Black lawmakers and voting rights advocates are expressing concern about a potential decline in minority political representation following oral arguments in a Supreme Court case.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown And Gary Fields, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: No SNAP benefits to be issued in November as government shutdown stretches on
item.description: Local governments are warning citizens that because the federal government has been shut down for weeks with no end in sight, November SNAP benefits will not be issued.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: First Republican enters race for governor of New Mexico in 2026 as Democrat terms out of office
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — New Mexico has its first Republican contender for governor ahead of the 2026 elections, as the three-term city mayor of fast-growing Rio Rancho launched his campaign.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House joins Bluesky and immediately trolls Trump opponents
item.description: The White House has joined Bluesky, the social media platform of choice of many in the left-leaning online world.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump offers US automakers additional relief from his tariffs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is giving domestic automakers additional relief from tariffs on auto parts, extending what was supposed to have been a short-term rebate until 2030.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to allow deployment of National Guard in Chicago area
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Friday asked the Supreme Court to allow the deployment of National Guard troops in the Chicago area, escalating President Donald Trump's conflict with Democratic governors over using the military on U.S. soil.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US has seized survivors after strike on suspected drug-carrying vessel in Caribbean, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States took survivors into custody after the military struck a suspected drug-carrying vessel in the Caribbean, a defense official and another person familiar with the matter said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From bills to berets: Notable moments from NYC's contentious mayoral debate
item.description: New York City's mayoral candidates duked it out in their first general election debate Thursday night, with contenders Zohran Mamdani, Andrew Cuomo and Curtis Sliwa sparring over everything from the Israel-Hamas war to the city's education system.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Bolton pleads not guilty
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senators see momentum on Russia sanctions even as Trump questions timing
item.description: Senate leaders push for vote on Russian sanctions as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy visits Washington to press for stronger action against Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FEMA staff sound the alarm on disaster preparedness at rally in front of agency headquarters
item.description: Current and former staff of the Federal Emergency Management Agency demonstrated against workforce and program cuts during a “FEMA Solidarity Rally” on Friday, a potentially risky act of protest because some of the same staffers were placed on leave after signing a public dissent letter in August.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says 'tremendous bad blood' between Zelenskyy, Putin delaying peace deal despite Middle East momentum
item.description: President Donald Trump said "tremendous bad blood" between Zelenskyy and Putin is delaying a peace deal from happening with the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thune slams Schumer's 'kingmaker' politics, refuses to 'kiss the ring' in shutdown talks
item.description: Republicans and Democrats remain deadlocked over Obamacare subsidies as Senate Majority Leader John Thune, R-S.D., promotes bottom-up leadership approach during shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats say Trump needs to be involved in shutdown talks. He's shown little interest in doing so
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is showing little urgency to broker a compromise that would end the government shutdown, even as Democrats insist no breakthrough is possible without his direct involvement. Three weeks in, Congress is at a standstill.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI and SEUNG MIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk ally Jared Isaacman makes comeback bid for NASA administrator role after May withdrawal: report
item.description: President Donald Trump is reconsidering Jared Isaacman for the NASA administrator role after previously pulling his nomination due to his ties to Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ahead of Zelenskyy meeting, Trump shows signs he might not be ready to send Kyiv Tomahawk missiles
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to host Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for talks at the White House on Friday. The U.S. leader is signaling he’s not ready to agree to sell Kyiv a long-range missile system that the Ukrainians say they desperately need.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Truck tariff could hit an already struggling industry hard
item.description: The tariffs will raise the cost of trucks and repairs, which could be passed on to the consumer in the form of higher prices.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bass, NewsNation

item.title: Thune to force Democrats to vote on paying the troops during the shutdown
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) plans to force Democrats to vote next week on legislation to pay more than 1 million military service members during the government shutdown, doubling down on his strategy of highlighting the plight of military families, according to Senate GOP sources.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta adding AI chatbot safety features for teens
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Instagram and Facebook, plans to roll out new safety features for its artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DNC chair: 'I believe that most Virginians have accepted' Jay Jones apology
item.description: Democratic National Committee (DNC) Chair Ken Martin said in an interview published Friday he believes most Virginians have accepted the apology from the party’s state attorney general nominee Jay Jones for recently unearthed violent text messages.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Parents of children with disabilities worry as Education Department shrinks
item.description: Parents of children with disabilities are growing increasingly worried as the Education Department shrinks and mechanisms for accountability seem harder to find.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tariffs hurting New Mexicans, Democrat says
item.description: SANTA TERESA, New Mexico (Border Report) – As a former secretary of the Interior during the Biden administration, Debra Haaland oversaw wildlife agencies and the conservation of public lands.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Indiana man accused of offering free homes in exchange for political help in Texas
item.description: LOVING COUNTY, TEXAS -- The Texas attorney general has brought a civil lawsuit against an Indiana man who is accused of offering housing to people if they help him "overthrow" the local government in a rural, sparsely populated county in the Lone Star State.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Haughn, NewsNation

item.title: Minneapolis mayoral candidates push for limits on ICE
item.description: Three challengers for the Minneapolis mayor’s seat say they support a ban on Minneapolis police sharing information with federal immigration agencies or providing crowd control for agencies like ICE.
item.dc:creator: Jon Collins, MPR News

item.title: Megyn Kelly says she'd run for president to stop Ocasio-Cortez
item.description: Conservative pundit Megyn Kelly said Thursday she could be motivated to run for president if Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) made her own White House bid. Laying into the progressive lawmaker on her popular podcast and radio show on Thursday, Kelly exclaimed "eff that woman.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Florida bill ignites debate: Teachers to pledge academic oath
item.description: A new bill just filed at the statehouse would make Florida educators take an oath to uphold the constitution and state laws before ever stepping foot into a classroom. It's similar to what lawyers, doctors, and public officials pledge.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: John Bolton indicted in classified documents case
item.description: The charges come two months after the FBI executed a search warrant at Bolton's suburban Washington home.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Make California an independent country? Fresno man's ballot initiative moves forward
item.description: Marcus Evans, a Fresno man, has moved one step closer to making California an independent country, as the California Secretary of State's Office has announced that the initiative can now start collecting petition signatures.
item.dc:creator: Dom McAndrew, Fox 5

item.title: Tim Walz, Tom Emmer feud ratchets up
item.description: Minnesota’s top Democrat and top Republican take frequent rhetorical jabs at one another although they’re not running against one another. The spat could have negative consequences for the people they jointly represent.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: The shutdown is good for business for flood insurance
item.description: With the government shutdown, the National Flood Insurance Program is no longer writing new policies. It's causing problems for would-be homeowners, but private companies have stepped in to help.
item.dc:creator: Greg Allen, MPR News

item.title: Government shutdown brings back memories for Minnesotan at center of 1995 shutdown
item.description: Annette Meeks, who was deputy chief of staff for Republican U.S. Rep. Newt Gingrich during the 21-day shutdown in 1995, spoke about government shutdowns with MPR News guest host Emily Bright on Morning Edition.
item.dc:creator: Emily Bright and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Prop 50 debate continues ahead of special election
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom is pushing Proposition 50, which would allow the state to gerrymander districts in response to Texas's recent changes, sparking debate over fair representation in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, Fox 5

item.title: Local leaders call for cities' ability to restrict guns
item.description: Local leaders say if legislators allow cities to act, they would seek bans on public possession of assault weapons; bans on devices that convert semiautomatic firearms into automatic firearms; and the ability to prohibit the possession of guns in public spaces like community centers and parks.
item.dc:creator: Jon Collins, MPR News

item.title: Dwindling paychecks add pressure on GOP, Trump, Democrats to end shutdown
item.description: Pressure on lawmakers to reach a deal to end the government shutdown is expected to rise this week with the prospect that workers will begin to lose paychecks, including members of the military.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WFLA, NBC
